intitule: Reading Comprehension: People and Aspects of Other Cultures

enonce.texte:
Read the biography below and answer the questions that follow.

Wangari Maathai: A Kenyan Hero

Wangari Maathai was born in Kenya in 1940. She was a famous environmental activist and the first African woman to win the Nobel Peace Prize. She founded the Green Belt Movement, which planted millions of trees in Africa. She believed that protecting the environment was important for peace. She always encouraged people to take care of the earth. Sometimes she faced challenges, but she never gave up. Now, many people around the world remember her as a hero. Her work has inspired people from different cultures to protect the environment.
images: An illustration of Wangari Maathai planting a tree with a group of women, with a green landscape in the background.

questions.texte: Identify the country where Wangari Maathai was born.
questions.reponse: Wangari Maathai was born in Kenya.

questions.texte: Explain why Wangari Maathai is famous.
questions.reponse: Wangari Maathai is famous because she was an environmental activist and the first African woman to win the Nobel Peace Prize.

questions.texte: Justify why Wangari Maathai believed that protecting the environment was important.
questions.reponse: Wangari Maathai believed that protecting the environment was important because she thought it was essential for peace.

questions.texte: Analyse the impact of Wangari Maathai's work on people from different cultures.
questions.reponse: Wangari Maathai's work has inspired people from different cultures to protect the environment. Her example shows that one person can make a difference. People around the world remember her as a hero, which shows that her message of environmental protection and peace transcends cultural boundaries.

enonce.texte: Complete the following sentences with the correct time adverbials from the box: now, then, later, sometimes, always, already.

questions.texte: Wangari Maathai __________ encouraged people to take care of the earth.
questions.reponse: Wangari Maathai always encouraged people to take care of the earth.

questions.texte: She founded the Green Belt Movement, and __________ she planted millions of trees.
questions.reponse: She founded the Green Belt Movement, and then she planted millions of trees.

questions.texte: I have __________ learned about Wangari Maathai in my class.
questions.reponse: I have already learned about Wangari Maathai in my class.

questions.texte: __________, many people around the world are inspired by her work.
questions.reponse: Now, many people around the world are inspired by her work.

questions.texte: __________ she faced challenges, but she never gave up.
questions.reponse: Sometimes she faced challenges, but she never gave up.

questions.texte: She completed her studies, and __________ she became an activist.
questions.reponse: She completed her studies, and later she became an activist.

intitule: Expressing Thoughts, Feelings, and Preferences about Other Cultures

enonce.texte: Complete the following sentences to express your thoughts, feelings, and preferences about people from other cultures.

questions.texte: I think it is __________ to learn about other cultures because __________.
questions.reponse: I think it is important to learn about other cultures because it helps us understand each other better.

questions.texte: I feel __________ when I meet people from other cultures because __________.
questions.reponse: I feel happy when I meet people from other cultures because I can learn new things from them.

questions.texte: I prefer to __________ when learning about other cultures.
questions.reponse: I prefer to read books and watch documentaries when learning about other cultures.

questions.texte: My favourite thing about other cultures is __________.
questions.reponse: My favourite thing about other cultures is their traditional food and music.

questions.texte: I believe that we should __________ to learn about other cultures.
questions.reponse: I believe that we should travel and meet people from other cultures to learn about them.

intitule: Writing: A Biography of a Person from Another Culture

enonce.texte:
Write a short biography (about 80-100 words) about a famous person from another culture that you admire.

Your biography should include:

  • The person's name and where they are from
  • What they are famous for
  • What you admire about them

Use at least three time adverbials (now, then, later, sometimes, always, already) in your biography.
images: A general illustration showing famous people from different cultures around the world, including activists, scientists, and leaders.

questions.reponse: Nelson Mandela was born in South Africa in 1918. He was a great leader who fought against apartheid. He always believed in equality and justice. He spent 27 years in prison. Later, he became the first black president of South Africa. Now, people around the world remember him as a symbol of peace and forgiveness. I admire him because he never gave up on his dream. His courage and determination continue to inspire people everywhere.

intitule: Writing: Expressing Preferences about Other Cultures

enonce.texte:
Write a short paragraph (about 60-80 words) expressing your thoughts, feelings, and preferences about people from other cultures.

Use at least two time adverbials in your paragraph.

questions.reponse: I think learning about people from other cultures is very exciting. I always enjoy discovering new traditions and customs. Sometimes I feel curious about how people live in other parts of the world. I prefer to learn about cultures through stories and music. I believe that every culture has something special to offer. Now, I try to make friends with people from different backgrounds. It makes me feel more connected to the world.

enonce.texte:
Write a short dialogue (at least 8 exchanges) between two students. One student is from Cameroon and the other is from another country. They are discussing cultural differences and similarities.

Use at least three time adverbials in the dialogue.

questions.reponse:
Marie: Hi! I'm Marie from Cameroon. Where are you from?
Pedro: Hello! I'm Pedro from Brazil. I'm new here.
Marie: Welcome! What do you think about Cameroon so far?
Pedro: It's beautiful! I like the food and the music. Sometimes I feel a little homesick though.
Marie: I understand. We have many cultural differences. For example, we greet elders differently here.
Pedro: That's interesting. In Brazil, we always greet people with hugs and kisses.
Marie: Here we often bow or shake hands to show respect.
Pedro: I'll remember that. Now I know how to greet people properly.
Marie: You'll learn more. Then you'll feel more comfortable.
Pedro: I already feel welcomed! Thank you for explaining.
